title: Everyone Is Special
lesson: Everyone looks different and likes different things, and those differences make us special friends.
prompts: In a bright cheerful classroom — a colourful alphabet rug, cubby shelves, warm light — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image sits cross-legged in a small circle, chatting and gesturing with one hand, with a friendly smile. Around them sit {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_BLACK}, {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_ASIAN} and {GENDER_M_AGE_06_RACE_SOUTH_ASIAN}, chatting together with cheerful, engaged expressions. Make the interaction read physically: each person faces the other person or the shared object, with hands and gaze directed to one connected action. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY}

In a bright cheerful classroom — a colourful alphabet rug, cubby shelves, warm light — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image sits, leaning forward with hands on their knees, with a curious, interested expression, watching a friend share. Nearby, {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_ASIAN} holds up a favourite picture book with a proud, sharing smile while {GENDER_M_AGE_06_RACE_SOUTH_ASIAN} leans in to look with an interested expression. Make the interaction read physically: each person faces the other person or the shared object, with hands and gaze directed to one connected action. If a prop is shared, show one shared prop linking their hands in one common space. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY}

In a bright cheerful classroom — a colourful alphabet rug, cubby shelves, warm light — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image sits proudly holding up their own favourite toy, with a happy, sharing smile. Nearby, {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_BLACK} and {GENDER_M_AGE_06_RACE_WHITE} clap and look interested. Exactly three children in the frame and no one else. Make the interaction read physically: each person faces the other person or the shared object, with hands and gaze directed to one connected action. If a prop is shared, show one shared prop linking their hands in one common space. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY}

In a bright cheerful classroom — a colourful alphabet rug, cubby shelves, warm light — four friends, and only four children in total, no extra or duplicate child, exactly four not five, stand in a single row noticing how different they all look. From left to right: first the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image, laughing and gesturing playfully, with a warm smile; then {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_BLACK}, who is the only child wearing red dungarees, with a delighted grin; then {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_ASIAN}, with a bright, curious smile; then {GENDER_M_AGE_06_RACE_SOUTH_ASIAN}, with a cheerful, proud expression. Each is one single distinct child, each looking different, laughing and waving. Make the interaction read physically: each person faces the other person or the shared object, with hands and gaze directed to one connected action. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY}

In a bright cheerful classroom — a colourful alphabet rug, cubby shelves, warm light — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image stands holding hands in a circle with friends, with a joyful smile. Nearby, {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_BLACK}, {GENDER_M_AGE_06_RACE_WHITE} and {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_ASIAN} hold hands too, all together, each with a happy, included expression. Make the interaction read physically: each person faces the other person or the shared object, with hands and gaze directed to one connected action. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY}

In a bright cheerful classroom — a colourful alphabet rug, cubby shelves, golden light — the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image stands in a happy group hug with friends, with a big warm smile. Nearby, {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_BLACK}, {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_ASIAN} and {GENDER_M_AGE_06_RACE_SOUTH_ASIAN} hug in close, all delighted. Exactly four children in the frame and no one else. Make the interaction read physically: each person faces the other person or the shared object, with hands and gaze directed to one connected action. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY}
gists: In a bright classroom on a colourful alphabet rug, the child sits cross-legged in a small circle, chatting and gesturing, while three other children sit chatting together.
On the classroom rug, the child leans forward with hands on knees, curious, watching a friend share; a girl holds up a favourite picture book while a boy leans in to look.
On the classroom rug, the child proudly holds up their own favourite toy with a sharing smile while two friends clap, interested — exactly three children.
On the classroom rug, four children stand in a single row noticing how different they all look — the child laughing and gesturing, then the three friends — exactly four, no duplicates.
On the classroom rug, the child holds hands in a circle with three friends, all joyful — different and special, side by side.
On the classroom rug in golden light, the child is in a happy group hug with three friends, all delighted — exactly four children. Everyone is special.
texts: {NAME} sat in a circle with three friends on the colourful classroom rug. Everyone was chatting happily. "I love story time!" said {NAME}.
One friend held up her favourite picture book. "This one is mine," she said. {NAME} leaned in, curious. "Ooh, I like that one too!"
Then {NAME} showed a favourite toy. "And this one is mine!" said {NAME} proudly. "Wow!" cheered the friends, clapping.
They all looked so different from one another. "You have curly hair, and I have glasses!" laughed {NAME}. Being different made them smile.
They held hands in one big circle, all together. "We're all friends," said {NAME}. Different and special, side by side.
Then came a great big group hug! "Everyone is special!" said {NAME}. And that made them the very best of friends.
